channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Gaza sisters win Earth Prize for turning war rubble into bricks
item.description: Palestinian sisters Tala and Farah Mousa turned the rubble of their bombed Gaza home into reusable bricks.

item.title: Outrage over Israel’s Ben-Gvir flotilla abuse video: What we know
item.description: In a video released by his office, National Security Minister Ben-Gvir taunts zip-tied activists, causing condemnation.

item.title: Canada is using its borders to police Palestine solidarity
item.description: Critics of Israel’s war on Gaza are increasingly facing interrogations, visa revocations and denials of entry.

item.title: How Chinese, Iranian companies profit in Russia-occupied Ukrainian regions
item.description: More than a dozen Chinese companies operate in Donetsk and Luhansk, according to a Ukrainian monitor.

item.title: Philippines orders arrest of fugitive senator wanted by ICC
item.description: Justice Secretary says anyone helping Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa evade arrest will 'face consequences'.

item.title: ‘Homeland or death’: How Cuba would defend itself against a US attack
item.description: A US military operation in Cuba could be imminent, but Havana is not entirely defenceless, some analysts argue.

item.title: Dropped Sardar Azmoun asserts his love for Iran, backs World Cup squad
item.description: Forward wishes 'success and pride' to the Iranian team after being excluded from the squad for the 2026 tournament.

item.title: What options do the US and Iran have left to bring war to an end?
item.description: What options do the US and Iran have left to bring war to an end?

item.title: Kite carrying Palestinian children’s messages reaches Mount Everest summit
item.description: Mountaineers carry a kite bearing messages and signatures from children in Gaza to the top of the world.

item.title: Flooding in southern China washes away cars and destroys bridge
item.description: Flooding in southern China washes away cars and destroys bridge

item.title: Ebola outbreak: When will a vaccine be developed for the new strain?
item.description: The Bundibugyo strain of Ebola has a fatality rate of up to 50 percent and no approved vaccine as yet.

item.title: Iran reviews US proposal to end war as Pakistan steps up mediation efforts
item.description: Pakistan military chief Asim Munir to travel to Tehran for talks, according to Iranian media reports.

item.title: ‘Iron brothers’: How China and Pakistan built an unlikely 75-year bond
item.description: From secret nuclear exchanges to a key diplomatic deal, Pakistan and China have long helped each other. What's next?

item.title: World Cup 2026: Spain ex-players, fans cautiously embrace favourites tag
item.description: Euro 2024 champions will feel the pressure of a football-mad nation as they aim to clinch World Cup-Euro double.

item.title: The economic shock of the Iran war will hit the world in four waves
item.description: The impact of the conflict will not stop at energy price hikes. It will spread to various sectors and last years.

item.title: Mohamed Salah to captain Egypt as squad announced for FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: Versatile Nantes forward Mostafa Mohamed is a significant omission from the 27-man preliminary squad.

item.title: Buddhist hall housing ‘eternal flame’ burns down in Japan
item.description: Buddhist hall housing ‘eternal flame’ burns down in Japan

item.title: Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited
item.description: Israeli officials and media indicate renewed conflict with Iran is being actively considered despite a shaky deadlock.

item.title: DR Congo cancel World Cup training camp in Kinshasa over Ebola outbreak
item.description: DRC's public sendoff in the capital was also cancelled before their departure for the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Taiwan’s President Lai says he would be happy to speak with Donald Trump
item.description: US and Taiwan leaders have not spoken since Washington shifted diplomatic recognition from Taipei to Beijing in 1979.

item.title: ‘King’ Emery hailed as Aston Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Europa League
item.description: Villa dominated Freiburg in the final in Istanbul to win their first trophy since 1996 and European title since 1982.

item.title: Walid Daqqa: A Parallel Life
item.description: Walid Daqqa, the Palestinian prisoner who turned decades in Israeli jails into moments of thought, creativity and love.

item.title: The numbers behind global mental health and its different disorders
item.description: Nearly one in eight people in the world lives with a mental disorder, and one person dies by suicide every 43 seconds.

item.title: Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque illuminated in Israeli flag colours
item.description: Palestinian officials condemned Israel’s illumination of Hebron’s Ibrahimi Mosque with Israeli flag colours and slogans.

item.title: Iran war day 83: Tehran ‘reviewing’ latest US response to end conflict
item.description: Iran says diplomacy with the US remains open as Hormuz tensions deepen and outrage grows over Israel’s flotilla arrests.
